title: QUIC RFC 9000 Reordering And Gap Immediate ACK Scheduling Architecture
purpose: Describe the bounded ACK-generation proof that reordered or gap-detecting ack-eliciting Application Data packets require immediate ACK scheduling to assist peer loss detection.
design_summary: ACK generation compares each new ack-eliciting packet number with the largest previously received ack-eliciting packet number in the same packet number space. A lower packet number indicates reordering, and a higher packet number separated by at least one missing packet indicates a gap. Either condition marks the space as requiring an immediate ACK, allowing outgoing packet composition to include an ACK frame before the ordinary delayed-ACK timer or packet-count threshold is reached. Contiguous ack-eliciting packets and non-ack-eliciting gap observations do not set this immediate flag.
data_and_state_considerations: This slice proves ACK scheduling state derived from processed packet numbers and ack-eliciting classification. It does not implement packet-number decoding, loss recovery, ACK range serialization, path validation, congestion response, or peer loss detection itself.
edge_cases_and_constraints: A lower ack-eliciting packet number than a previously received ack-eliciting packet requires immediate ACK scheduling.
A higher ack-eliciting packet number with a missing packet between the previous largest and the new packet requires immediate ACK scheduling.
A contiguous ack-eliciting packet remains on the ordinary delayed-ACK path.
A non-ack-eliciting packet that creates a numeric gap does not use this ack-eliciting-only trigger.
Deterministic fuzz/property coverage samples duplicate, lower, contiguous, and gapped second-packet observations.
alternatives_considered: Rely on broad RFC 9000 trace only; rejected because the empty requirement home did not directly prove either immediate-ACK branch.
Combine the slice with ACK range encoding or peer loss recovery; rejected because those are separate behavior surfaces with their own proof burden.
Treat all numeric gaps as immediate ACK triggers; rejected because the RFC rule is scoped to ack-eliciting packets.
risks: Overstating this proof as sender-side loss detection would blur receiver ACK scheduling and peer recovery responsibilities.
Triggering on non-ack-eliciting packets would weaken feedback-loop protections.
Missing the lower-packet branch would leave reordering evidence delayed until the normal ACK schedule.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This design covers only receiver-side ACK scheduling once packet processing has supplied packet numbers and ack-eliciting classification. It does not claim packet-number decoding, ACK frame serialization, loss recovery, congestion response, endpoint interop, or public API behavior.
